String.signature: public sealed class String : IComparable, IEnumerable<char>, IEquatable<String>
String.description: Represents text as a sequence of UTF-16 code units. Strings are immutable.

Length.description: Gets the number of characters in the current String object.

Substring.signature: public string Substring(int startIndex, int length)
Substring.description: Retrieves a substring from this instance starting at a specified character position and having a specified length.

Contains.signature: public bool Contains(string value, StringComparison comparisonType)
Contains.description: Returns a value indicating whether a specified substring occurs within this string, using the specified comparison rules.

StartsWith.signature: public bool StartsWith(string value, StringComparison comparisonType)
StartsWith.description: Determines whether the beginning of this string instance matches the specified string.

EndsWith.signature: public bool EndsWith(string value, StringComparison comparisonType)
EndsWith.description: Determines whether the end of this string instance matches the specified string.

IndexOf.signature: public int IndexOf(string value, StringComparison comparisonType)
IndexOf.description: Reports the zero-based index of the first occurrence of the specified string. Returns -1 if not found.

LastIndexOf.signature: public int LastIndexOf(string value, StringComparison comparisonType)
LastIndexOf.description: Reports the zero-based index position of the last occurrence of a specified string. Returns -1 if not found.

Replace.signature: public string Replace(string oldValue, string? newValue)
Replace.description: Returns a new string in which all occurrences of a specified string are replaced with another specified string.

Split.signature: public string[] Split(char separator, StringSplitOptions options = None)
Split.description: Splits a string into substrings based on specified delimiting characters and options.

Trim.description: Removes all leading and trailing white-space characters from the current string.

TrimStart.description: Removes all leading white-space characters from the current string.

TrimEnd.description: Removes all trailing white-space characters from the current string.

ToUpper.description: Returns a copy of this string converted to uppercase using the casing rules of the current culture.

ToLower.description: Returns a copy of this string converted to lowercase using the casing rules of the current culture.

PadLeft.signature: public string PadLeft(int totalWidth, char paddingChar = ' ')
PadLeft.description: Returns a new string that right-aligns the characters in this instance by padding them on the left with a specified character.

PadRight.signature: public string PadRight(int totalWidth, char paddingChar = ' ')
PadRight.description: Returns a new string that left-aligns the characters in this string by padding them on the right with a specified character.

Insert.signature: public string Insert(int startIndex, string value)
Insert.description: Returns a new string in which a specified string is inserted at a specified index position in this instance.

Remove.signature: public string Remove(int startIndex, int count)
Remove.description: Returns a new string in which a specified number of characters beginning at a specified position have been deleted.

Equals.signature: public bool Equals(string? value, StringComparison comparisonType)
Equals.description: Determines whether this string and a specified String object have the same value, using the specified comparison rules.

CompareTo.description: Compares this instance with a specified String object and indicates whether this instance precedes, follows, or appears in the same position in the sort order.

IsNullOrEmpty.description: Indicates whether the specified string is null or an empty string ("").

IsNullOrWhiteSpace.description: Indicates whether a specified string is null, empty, or consists only of white-space characters.

Format.signature: public static string Format(string format, params object?[] args)
Format.description: Replaces one or more format items in a string with the string representation of a specified object.

Join.signature: public static string Join(string? separator, IEnumerable<string?> values)
Join.description: Concatenates the members of a collection, using the specified separator between each member.

Concat.signature: public static string Concat(params string?[] values)
Concat.description: Concatenates one or more instances of String.

List.description: Represents a strongly typed list of objects that can be accessed by index. Provides methods to search, sort, and manipulate lists.

Add.description: Adds an object to the end of the List<T>.

AddRange.description: Adds the elements of the specified collection to the end of the List<T>.

Insert.signature: public void Insert(int index, T item)
Insert.description: Inserts an element into the List<T> at the specified index.

Remove.description: Removes the first occurrence of a specific object from the List<T>. Returns true if the item was successfully removed.

RemoveAt.description: Removes the element at the specified index of the List<T>.

RemoveAll.description: Removes all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate. Returns the number of elements removed.

Clear.description: Removes all elements from the List<T>.

Contains.description: Determines whether an element is in the List<T>. Uses the default equality comparer.

IndexOf.description: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence. Returns -1 if not found.

Find.description: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the first occurrence.

FindAll.description: Retrieves all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

FindIndex.description: Searches for an element that matches the predicate and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence. Returns -1 if not found.

Sort.description: Sorts the elements in the entire List<T> using the specified comparison.

Reverse.description: Reverses the order of the elements in the entire List<T>.

Count.description: Gets the number of elements contained in the List<T>.

ForEach.description: Performs the specified action on each element of the List<T>.

ToArray.description: Copies the elements of the List<T> to a new array.

GetRange.signature: public List<T> GetRange(int index, int count)
GetRange.description: Creates a shallow copy of a range of elements in the source List<T>.

BinarySearch.description: Searches the entire sorted List<T> for an element using the default comparer and returns the zero-based index.

Exists.description: Determines whether the List<T> contains elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

TrueForAll.description: Determines whether every element in the List<T> matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

ConvertAll.description: Converts the elements in the current List<T> to another type, and returns a list containing the converted elements.

Dictionary.signature: public class Dictionary<TKey, TValue> : IDictionary<TKey, TValue>, IReadOnlyDictionary<TKey, TValue> where TKey : notnull
Dictionary.description: Represents a collection of keys and values. Provides O(1) average-time complexity for lookups, insertions, and removals.

Add.signature: public void Add(TKey key, TValue value)
Add.description: Adds the specified key and value to the dictionary. Throws ArgumentException if the key already exists.

Remove.description: Removes the value with the specified key from the dictionary. Returns true if the element was successfully found and removed.

ContainsKey.description: Determines whether the Dictionary<TKey,TValue> contains the specified key.

ContainsValue.description: Determines whether the Dictionary<TKey,TValue> contains a specific value. This is an O(n) operation.

TryGetValue.signature: public bool TryGetValue(TKey key, out TValue value)
TryGetValue.description: Gets the value associated with the specified key. Returns true if the key was found.

Keys.description: Gets a collection containing the keys in the dictionary.

Values.description: Gets a collection containing the values in the dictionary.

Count.description: Gets the number of key/value pairs contained in the dictionary.

Clear.description: Removes all keys and values from the dictionary.

TryAdd.signature: public bool TryAdd(TKey key, TValue value)
TryAdd.description: Attempts to add the specified key and value to the dictionary. Returns false if the key already exists (does not throw).

GetValueOrDefault.signature: public TValue GetValueOrDefault(TKey key, TValue defaultValue)
GetValueOrDefault.description: Gets the value associated with the specified key, or a default value if the key is not present.

Task.description: Represents an asynchronous operation. The Task class provides a way to schedule work and retrieve results or exceptions from asynchronous operations.

Run.signature:
public static Task Run(Action action)
public static Task<TResult> Run<TResult>(Func<TResult> function)
Run.description: Queues the specified work to run on the ThreadPool and returns a Task or Task<TResult> representing that work.

WhenAll.signature:
public static Task WhenAll(IEnumerable<Task> tasks)
public static Task<TResult[]> WhenAll<TResult>(IEnumerable<Task<TResult>> tasks)
WhenAll.description: Creates a task that will complete when all of the supplied tasks have completed.

WhenAny.description: Creates a task that will complete when any of the supplied tasks have completed.

Delay.signature:
public static Task Delay(int millisecondsDelay)
public static Task Delay(TimeSpan delay)
Delay.description: Creates a task that completes after a specified time interval.

FromResult.description: Creates a Task<TResult> that is completed successfully with the specified result.

ContinueWith.description: Creates a continuation that executes asynchronously when the target Task completes.

ConfigureAwait.description: Configures an awaiter used to await this Task. Set to false to avoid marshalling back to the original synchronization context.

GetAwaiter.description: Gets an awaiter used to await this Task. Used by the compiler to implement the await keyword.

Result.description: Gets the result value of the Task<TResult>. Blocks the calling thread until the task completes if not already finished.

IsCompleted.description: Gets whether this Task has completed (successfully, faulted, or canceled).

IsFaulted.description: Gets whether the Task completed due to an unhandled exception.

Exception.description: Gets the AggregateException that caused the Task to end prematurely. Returns null if the Task completed successfully or was canceled.

Enumerable.description: Provides a set of static methods for querying objects that implement IEnumerable<T>. This is the core class for LINQ to Objects.

Where.signature: public static IEnumerable<TSource> Where<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, bool> predicate)
Where.description: Filters a sequence of values based on a predicate.

Select.signature: public static IEnumerable<TResult> Select<TSource, TResult>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, TResult> selector)
Select.description: Projects each element of a sequence into a new form.

SelectMany.signature: public static IEnumerable<TResult> SelectMany<TSource, TResult>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, IEnumerable<TResult>> selector)
SelectMany.description: Projects each element of a sequence to an IEnumerable<T> and flattens the resulting sequences into one sequence.

OrderBy.signature: public static IOrderedEnumerable<TSource> OrderBy<TSource, TKey>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, TKey> keySelector)
OrderBy.description: Sorts the elements of a sequence in ascending order according to a key.

OrderByDescending.signature: public static IOrderedEnumerable<TSource> OrderByDescending<TSource, TKey>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, TKey> keySelector)
OrderByDescending.description: Sorts the elements of a sequence in descending order according to a key.

GroupBy.signature: public static IEnumerable<IGrouping<TKey, TSource>> GroupBy<TSource, TKey>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, TKey> keySelector)
GroupBy.description: Groups the elements of a sequence according to a specified key selector function.

First.signature: public static TSource First<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
First.description: Returns the first element of a sequence. Throws InvalidOperationException if the sequence is empty.

FirstOrDefault.signature: public static TSource? FirstOrDefault<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
FirstOrDefault.description: Returns the first element of a sequence, or a default value if the sequence contains no elements.

Single.signature: public static TSource Single<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
Single.description: Returns the only element of a sequence. Throws if there is not exactly one element.

SingleOrDefault.signature: public static TSource? SingleOrDefault<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
SingleOrDefault.description: Returns the only element, or a default value if the sequence is empty. Throws if more than one element.

Any.signature: public static bool Any<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, bool> predicate)
Any.description: Determines whether any element of a sequence satisfies a condition.

All.signature: public static bool All<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, bool> predicate)
All.description: Determines whether all elements of a sequence satisfy a condition.

Count.signature: public static int Count<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
Count.description: Returns the number of elements in a sequence.

Sum.signature: public static int Sum(this IEnumerable<int> source)
Sum.description: Computes the sum of a sequence of numeric values.

Min.signature: public static TSource? Min<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
Min.description: Returns the minimum value in a sequence.

Max.signature: public static TSource? Max<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
Max.description: Returns the maximum value in a sequence.

Average.signature: public static double Average(this IEnumerable<int> source)
Average.description: Computes the average of a sequence of numeric values.

Aggregate.signature: public static TSource Aggregate<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, TSource, TSource> func)
Aggregate.description: Applies an accumulator function over a sequence.

Distinct.signature: public static IEnumerable<TSource> Distinct<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
Distinct.description: Returns distinct elements from a sequence by using the default equality comparer.

Take.signature: public static IEnumerable<TSource> Take<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, int count)
Take.description: Returns a specified number of contiguous elements from the start of a sequence.

Skip.signature: public static IEnumerable<TSource> Skip<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, int count)
Skip.description: Bypasses a specified number of elements in a sequence and then returns the remaining elements.

ToList.signature: public static List<TSource> ToList<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
ToList.description: Creates a List<T> from an IEnumerable<T>.

ToArray.signature: public static TSource[] ToArray<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source)
ToArray.description: Creates an array from an IEnumerable<T>.

ToDictionary.signature: public static Dictionary<TKey, TSource> ToDictionary<TSource, TKey>(this IEnumerable<TSource> source, Func<TSource, TKey> keySelector) where TKey : notnull
ToDictionary.description: Creates a Dictionary<TKey,TValue> from an IEnumerable<T> according to a specified key selector function.

Range.signature: public static IEnumerable<int> Range(int start, int count)
Range.description: Generates a sequence of integral numbers within a specified range.

Empty.description: Returns an empty IEnumerable<T> that has the specified type argument.

Repeat.signature: public static IEnumerable<TResult> Repeat<TResult>(TResult element, int count)
Repeat.description: Generates a sequence that contains one repeated value.

Zip.signature: public static IEnumerable<(TFirst, TSecond)> Zip<TFirst, TSecond>(this IEnumerable<TFirst> first, IEnumerable<TSecond> second)
Zip.description: Produces a sequence of tuples with elements from the two specified sequences.

Concat.signature: public static IEnumerable<TSource> Concat<TSource>(this IEnumerable<TSource> first, IEnumerable<TSource> second)

IEnumerable.description: Exposes an enumerator for iterating over a collection

StringBuilder.description: Mutable string for efficient string manipulation

Exception.description: Base class for errors and exceptions

Span.description: Type-safe representation of a contiguous region of memory

HttpClient.description: Class for sending HTTP requests and receiving responses

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
